name: HWM_TM_REC_CHANGES
description: Table to store changes for time record or time events.

columns.Name: SECONDARY_TM_REC_ID
columns.Comments: Time Record Id to store start time entry for OUT_IN Event type.

columns.Name: SECONDARY_TM_REC_VER
columns.Comments: Time Record Version to store start time entry for OUT_IN Event type.

columns.Name: TM_REC_ID
columns.Comments: Time record that needs to be updated, for new time records, this will be null.

columns.Name: TM_REC_VERSION
columns.Comments: Version of the time record that needs to be updated, it will be used for tracking concurrent updates

columns.Name: TM_EVENT_ID
columns.Comments: Time event id in case the request is created on time event.

columns.Name: CRUD_STATUS
columns.Comments: Action being performed on the record, we will use the enum that we have.

columns.Name: PROCESS_STATUS
columns.Comments: Status of the event  (SUBMITTED, SUBMITTED PENDING INTERNALIZATION, APPROVED, APPROVED PENDING INTERNALIZATION, REJECTED, CANCELLED, IN-PROCESS, COMPLETE)

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: COMMENTS
columns.Comments: Used to store justification of the change.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.
